goal: Extend the /smoke-test skill with multi-weight support (lightweight + medium), worktree isolation, Option B gate simulation in pipeline docs, and concrete test cases per weight class

risks: Option B gate simulation in 04b-clarification.md and 07-plan-approval.md must run the FULL gate logic before defaulting — if the test_mode check short-circuits too early, the test won't exercise the real code path and gives false confidence.

Worktree isolation adds complexity to cleanup. If the worktree remove fails (e.g., locked files, agent still writing), orphan worktrees accumulate in /tmp. The trap cleanup must handle this gracefully with fallback to manual cleanup instructions.

Lightweight skip set differs from medium (lightweight skips challenge + brainstorm; medium skips brainstorm only). The weight-dependent skip logic in run-smoke-test.sh must match the actual pipeline skip rules exactly or the test will report false failures for legitimately skipped steps.
over_engineering_flags: Do NOT build a test case definition DSL or config file for weight-specific behaviors. Hard-code the two concrete test cases (marker file for lightweight, utility module for medium) directly in the bash script.
Do NOT add heavyweight support. The CEO explicitly deferred it. Only lightweight and medium.

projects.title: Multi-weight smoke test with worktree isolation and gate simulation
projects.scope_summary: Extend run-smoke-test.sh with: (1) weight argument ($1 = lightweight|medium, default medium), weight-dependent skip set and test case selection; (2) git worktree isolation replacing branch checkout; (3) concrete test cases — lightweight creates a marker file, medium creates a utility module with function + config. Extend SKILL.md to pass $ARGUMENTS as weight. Add test_mode check to 04b-clarification.md (run full synthesis then auto-respond) and 07-plan-approval.md (run full plan presentation then auto-approve). All 4 extend items are tightly coupled — same files, same domain.
